name: Wire Splice Point Delta-7, No Man's Land
description: A specific point in the crater field, marked on Kowalski's hand-drawn wire chart as D-7, where the forward copper telephone line was cut. The cut is clean, diagonal, and sits in a shallow depression between two overlapping shell craters, sheltered from casual observation.
atmosphericDetails: The two ends of the copper wire lie in the mud three feet apart; the cut surfaces are bright and unoxidized, indicating they were made within the last two hours
A bootprint in the clay beside the cut is deeper on the left heel — the print of someone who paused here, shifted their weight, worked with both hands
A single strand of khaki wool thread is caught on the wire's cut end — the color matches American officer's uniform fabric, not enlisted
